title: bidmonkey quiz authoring YAML (v1)
description:
Validates a hand-authored quiz YAML file (one source + one quiz + its problems) BEFORE normalization. This is deliberately a THIN structural layer — shape, required keys, enums, and token regexes — so an editor can flag typos live. All domain rules that JSON Schema cannot express live in the Python normalizer/validator, and are listed under `x-deferred-checks` below.

The authoring format is shorthand: case-insensitive, unicode suit symbols allowed, hands written suit-then-ranks (`caqj97`), single cards in `plays` written rank-then-suit (`3C`). The normalizer folds all of this into the canonical stored shape (see schema.v1.json).
x-deferred-checks: hand integrity: 4 suits in SHDC order, valid ranks, exactly 13 cards, no duplicate card within a hand
cross-hand: no card appears in two hands; `hands` seats unique after case-folding
auction: starts at dealer and proceeds clockwise, at most one skipped seat between shown calls; bids strictly ascending; X only over an opponent's live suit/NT bid; XX only over an opponent's X
auction: `answer`/`accept` are legal calls at that point in the auction
every `?` step's seat equals `player` (auctions and plays)
each auction/play step has exactly one seat key
vuln normalized to absolute NS/EW using `player`; `us`/`them` resolved via the player's side
contract asserted against the auction's computed final contract and declarer
plays: opening leader is LHO of declarer; each card is in that seat's hand; follow-suit legal (when all four hands are present); no card played twice; trick winner leads next
`all: p` expanded; skipped seats filled with P; full auction echoed back for review

source.description: Source as `Title [slug]`; created if it doesn't exist.

quiz.description: Quiz as `Title [slug]`; created if it doesn't exist.

titles.description: Template for per-problem titles; `#` is replaced with `#` + the 1-based ordinal (e.g. `SAYC No Trump #` -> `SAYC No Trump #3`).

direction.description: A seat: N, E, S, or W (any case).

handString.description: A whole hand as space-separated suit tokens, suit-then-ranks, e.g. `sa65 hkq2 d53 caqj97`. Suit = S/H/D/C or a unicode pip. Ranks use A K Q J, 10 or T for ten, 2-9. A void suit is the suit letter + `-` (e.g. `s-`). Suit order (SHDC) and the 13-card total are checked by the normalizer, not here.

call.description: A call: level 1-7 + strain (C/D/H/S, N or NT, or a unicode pip); P/pass; X (double); XX (redouble). A trailing `*` marks an alert and is ignored.

anyOf.description: A question posed to the player at this call.

card.description: A single card, rank-then-suit, e.g. `3C`, `AC`, `10D` (note: the reverse of the suit-then-ranks order used in hands).

anyOf.description: A question posed to the player at this play.

vuln.description: Vulnerability. Player-relative: `none`/`-`/`love`, `us`, `them`, `both`/`all`. Seat-absolute: `ns`, `ew`. Normalized to absolute NS/EW using `player`. Absent => null.

contract.description: Final contract as level+strain (+optional X/XX) + declarer seat, e.g. `3NT W`. Checked against the auction by the normalizer.

bidStep.description: One auction step: exactly one seat key (N/E/S/W, or `all` for `all: p`) whose value is a call or `?`. A `?` step also carries `explain`/`answer`, plus optional `choices` (a list => multiple choice; omitted => free bid entry) and `accept`. (Exactly-one-seat, answer-required-for-?, and answer/accept ⊆ choices are enforced by the normalizer.)

choices.description: Multiple-choice options (in display order). Omit for free entry.

cardStep.description: One play: exactly one seat key (N/E/S/W) whose value is a card or `?`. A `?` step also carries `explain`/`answer`, plus optional `choices` (a list => multiple choice; omitted => free card entry) and `accept` (answers/choices are cards).

choices.description: Multiple-choice options (in display order). Omit for free entry.

hand.description: A single hand (the player's), when only one hand is shown.

hands.description: Multiple hands, keyed by seat (a map, so seats can't repeat). A value of `-` marks a seat intentionally omitted.

auction.description: Ordered calls from the dealer, clockwise. Defaults to empty.

plays.description: Ordered plays from the opening leader (LHO of declarer). No play may be skipped.
